task.json metadata, task description line 3 states 'achieve a maximum thickness of approximately 15% of the chord'. Verified against NACA airfoil standards (NACA 0015 is a symmetric airfoil with 15% thickness-to-chord ratio), confirmed by Web search results on NACA airfoil nomenclature and Wikipedia

pi_items.verified_value: Direct Foil Design (from XFLR5, integrated in QBlade v0.96)
pi_items.source: task.json description references 'Direct Foil Design' module. Verified through Web search results: XFLR5 forum and documentation show 'Direct Foil Design' is a known design module in XFLR5 with spline control points. QBlade v0.96 is based on XFLR5 (as documented in Web search results on QBlade integration with XFOIL/XFLR5)

pi_items.verified_value: Symmetric airfoil (upper and lower surfaces are mirror images)
pi_items.source: task.json description line 2: 'use the spline control points to design a symmetric airfoil (upper and lower surfaces should be mirror images)'. Verified against NACA airfoil standards - NACA 0015 designation with leading zeros (00) indicates zero camber = symmetric design

pi_items.verified_value: .wpa (QBlade Project Archive) - legacy text-based project format for QBlade v0.96
pi_items.source: QBlade documentation Web search results confirm .wpa is the project file format for QBlade v0.96. Note: Modern QBlade 2.0+ uses .qpr binary format, but v0.96 uses .wpa text format

privileged_info_summary: This task requires users to create a custom symmetric airfoil with 15% thickness using QBlade v0.96's Direct Foil Design module (integrated from XFLR5). The airfoil should be named 'SymCustom15' and saved as a .wpa project file named 'custom_spline_foil.wpa' in the /home/ga/Documents/projects/ directory. All metadata values are verified: the 15% thickness target matches NACA naming conventions (symmetric airfoils with camber=0 are denoted by 00XX where XX is the thickness percent), .wpa is the correct project file format for QBlade v0.96, the 500-byte minimum file size is reasonable for airfoil coordinate data (sample airfoil files in the workspace range 693-1499 bytes), and Direct Foil Design is a legitimate module within XFLR5 that QBlade v0.96 integrates. The task data is synthetic (user-created during the task execution).
